standards.title: Institutional Integrity
standards.vulnerability: Systemic Candor Breakdown. Omitting mandatory SACSCOC approval contingencies from official press releases; concealing the April 2026 Academic Referral Report.
standards.strategy: Scrub detailed dockets; run a multi-semester blanket agenda scheme to project an illusion of total control.

standards.title: Institutional Mission
standards.vulnerability: Mission Subversion. Unilaterally abandoning the traditional liberal arts HBCU mandate to force a compressed polytechnic blueprint without a 5-year cyclical evaluation.
standards.strategy: Package radical transformations under a generic 'Academic Plan' heading to bypass public mission alteration reviews.

standards.title: Continuous Operation
standards.vulnerability: Operation Interruption. Slashing core degree programs unilaterally under SB 185 before SACSCOC evaluates or authorizes teach-out frameworks.
standards.strategy: Mask rapid programmatic shutdowns under standard committee headings to dodge immediate teach-out and enrollment scrutiny.

standards.title: Fiduciary Oversight & Distinction
standards.vulnerability: Fiduciary Abandonment. Authorizing a 'Proposed Farm Purchase' despite a -$59M deficit; stuffing adverse compliance failure audits into non-debatable blocks.
standards.strategy: Bury the devastating CLA Single Audit inside the Consent Agenda; approve macro capital expansions via unitemized blanket votes.

standards.title: Control of Intercollegiate Athletics
standards.vulnerability: Athletic Budget Siphoning. Executing unmonitored internal pool raids ($29.5M) to patch baseline operating payroll; siphoning academic dollars to mask overruns.
standards.strategy: Omit cost-versus-income athletic ledger comparisons from public files; route asset trace lines behind a closed executive session firewall.

standards.title: Archived Information
standards.vulnerability: Document Spoliation & Catalog Tampering. Wiping or breaking historical public catalogs and degree tracks to obscure the scale of academic cuts executed under SB 185.
standards.strategy: Break digital archive links; let network proxies obscure historical degree tracking structures to stop verification of breached contracts.

standards.title: Library & Information Resources
standards.vulnerability: Starved Learning Infrastructure. Diverting designated student library and technology fees into central treasury cash flows; allowing core database subscriptions to expire.
standards.strategy: Mask severe library resource depletion and expired database subscriptions under generic 'Faculty Workload' and restructuring headings.
